questionHtml:
Which factors affect the amount of product formed at the cathode during electrolysis of molten salts?

    I. current
    II. time
    III. charge on the cation

A. I and II only

B. I and III only

C. II and III only

D. I, II and III

meta.title: Which factors affect the amount of product formed at the cathode during electrolysis of molten salts? I. current II. time III. charge on the cation A. I and II only B. I and III on
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.2.8—An electrolytic cell is an electrochemical cell that converts electrical energy to chemical energy by bringing about non-spontaneous reactions. Explain how current is conducted in an electrolytic cell. Deduce the products of the electrolysis of a molten salt.
